title: Tool Design, Errors, Pagination, and Context Efficiency · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: Refine an MCP server whose tools are ambiguous and return too much data. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for an efficient tool API and evaluation suite. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose tool granularity that maps to meaningful user intent without unsafe generic execution.
Write discriminative descriptions, constrained schemas, examples, and side-effect metadata.
Return actionable domain errors without exposing secrets or stack internals.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""Local MCP protocol and tool-contract portfolio."""

# Add typed, bounded protocol increments below.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Tool Granularity evidence cycle before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Tool Granularity assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Choose tool granularity that maps to meaningful user intent without unsafe generic execution. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: agent ext tool quality. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Tool Granularity, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to an efficient tool API and evaluation suite.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: More tiny tools always improve selection. This fails the Tool Granularity contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: OpenAI Codex Manual: Build Skills (Verified current 2026-07-13) — Controls Codex skill structure, progressive disclosure, locations, invocation, plugins, and dependencies.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose tool granularity that maps to meaningful user intent without unsafe generic execution.

Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

options.text: Run the boundary case, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.
Accept the result because visible output is the highest authority.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Tool Evals applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Evaluate selection, argument construction, recovery, context cost, and side-effect accuracy.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The decision applies Tool Granularity to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Tool Granularity.
